proposed_title: Spalling in Concrete and Pavement Surfaces
meta_description: Spalling is the breaking, chipping, or flaking of concrete or asphalt surface at joints, edges, or cracks, often exposing aggregate or reinforcing steel. In airport and road inspection, spalling is a critical structural defect indicating moisture ingress, freeze-thaw damage, or corrosion-driven delamination. Covers severity classification from AASHTO element inspection, FHWA LTPP measurement methods, and image-based detection approaches.

proposed_title: Efflorescence on Concrete and Masonry Surfaces
meta_description: Efflorescence is the white crystalline deposit of water-soluble salts on concrete and masonry surfaces, formed when water migrates through the material and evaporates. In structural inspection, efflorescence signals moisture penetration, potential internal deterioration, and conditions favorable for reinforcement corrosion. Covers chemical formation, visual detection by AI, severity implications, and differentiation from other white surface deposits.

proposed_title: Rutting in Asphalt and Flexible Pavements
